Required before using MessageX

MessageX Terms of Service & Safety Agreement

Please read this detailed agreement before entering. MessageX includes private and group messaging, media uploads, audio/video calls, screen sharing, games, polls, profiles, themes, browser/device storage, and an optional location-aware Live Weather theme. By accepting, you agree to use MessageX responsibly and understand the risks below.
Plain-language summary: Be at least 13. Get parent/guardian permission if required. Do not bully, threaten, scam, impersonate, expose private information, share illegal content, or use MessageX for emergencies. Do not share sensitive information. Calls and screen sharing can reveal your camera, microphone, surroundings, notifications, apps, and private data. MessageX may not stop every harmful message or user. MessageX can experience data leaks, database exposure, account compromise, device compromise, or accidental disclosure. This version does not use a full dedicated authenticator or advanced authentication system such as Supabase Auth, OAuth, passkeys, authenticator-app MFA, SMS MFA, email verification, or enterprise-grade identity protection. The optional Live Weather theme may request device location. If you separately opt in below, it may use your public IP address through an IP-location provider to estimate your city/region when device location is denied or unavailable.

1. Acceptance of these Terms

These Terms of Service & Safety Agreement apply whenever you access or use MessageX, including messages, profiles, calls, group calls, screen sharing, games, polls, media uploads, themes, and any future features added to the app. If you do not agree, do not use MessageX.

By checking the boxes and selecting I Accept, you confirm that you read these Terms, understand them, and agree to follow them. Your acceptance may be saved in this browser or device so the app can remember that you accepted.

2. Age requirement and parent/guardian permission

You must be at least 13 years old to use MessageX. If you are under 13, you are not allowed to use MessageX. If you are under 18, you should only use MessageX with permission from a parent or legal guardian and must follow any rules your parent, guardian, school, or household sets for online communication.

If you lie about your age, bypass this screen, use another person’s account, or help someone under 13 use MessageX, your access may be limited, blocked, or removed.

3. Account responsibility

You are responsible for what happens from your account. This includes your username, profile, avatar, bio, messages, uploads, reactions, calls, screen sharing, games, polls, settings, and any actions taken while your account is signed in.

  • Use a username and profile that do not expose your full legal name, school, address, phone number, or other sensitive details.
  • Keep your password private. Do not share your password with friends, classmates, strangers, or people pretending to help you.
  • Do not use the same password you use for important accounts like email, banking, school, or family accounts.
  • If you think someone else accessed your account, stop using it and change your password or contact the app owner/admin.

4. Important password, authenticator, and security warning

Security warning: This version of MessageX is a custom web app and may not provide the same account security as major commercial messaging platforms. It does not use a full dedicated authenticator or advanced authentication system such as Supabase Auth, OAuth, passkeys, authenticator-app multi-factor authentication, SMS/email verification, hardware security keys, enterprise identity management, automatic suspicious-login detection, or guaranteed encrypted credential handling. Do not treat it like a bank, medical portal, school records system, secure identity system, legal records system, or confidential business platform. Do not reuse important passwords here.

MessageX may rely on browser storage, device storage, Supabase/database storage, simple username/password checks, internet connections, app code, storage buckets, and browser/device permissions to save or access account data, messages, settings, profiles, media, calls, or game activity. No system is perfectly secure. Bugs, misconfiguration, weak passwords, shared devices, browser extensions, screenshots, screen recordings, database rules, exposed API keys, permissive Row Level Security policies, storage bucket mistakes, cached files, or device access can expose information.

Because this app does not currently use a stronger authenticator flow, users should understand that account protection may be limited. The app may not reliably verify a user’s real identity, may not prevent password sharing, may not stop someone who knows or guesses a password, may not detect suspicious logins, may not force password resets, may not confirm email ownership, and may not protect against all unauthorized access. If someone else uses your device, knows your password, accesses your browser storage, or gets access to the database, your account or content may be exposed.

  • Do not use a password from your email, school, bank, gaming, social media, family, or work accounts.
  • Do not share passwords, login details, recovery codes, screenshots of settings, or private account information.
  • Do not assume that a username proves someone’s real identity.
  • Do not send sensitive, secret, embarrassing, financial, medical, legal, school, or identity information through MessageX.
  • Use MessageX only with the understanding that accounts and data may be less protected than on professionally managed messaging platforms.

4A. Data leak and exposure risks

Data leak warning: MessageX can experience accidental or unauthorized data exposure. A data leak could reveal usernames, profiles, bios, avatars, messages, media, reactions, polls, game activity, call records, presence/online status, settings, uploaded files, storage URLs, cached content, or other app data.

Data can leak or be exposed through many causes, including coding mistakes, database misconfiguration, weak or missing access rules, overly permissive Supabase policies, exposed project keys, public storage buckets, shared devices, browser cache, localStorage, IndexedDB, backups, logs, screenshots, screen recordings, compromised accounts, malicious users, browser extensions, malware, lost devices, or someone with access to the app owner/admin tools.

You should use MessageX as if messages and uploads could eventually be seen by unintended people. Even if a feature looks private, do not rely on it for secrets. MessageX cannot promise that data will never be leaked, copied, viewed by another person, accessed by an admin, exposed by a bug, or retained somewhere after deletion.

5. Privacy limits

You should assume that anything you type, upload, show on camera, say on microphone, share through screen share, or display in your profile could be copied, screenshotted, recorded, forwarded, downloaded, or shown to someone else by another user. MessageX cannot fully prevent screenshots, screen recording, another device filming the screen, or someone repeating what you said.

Do not share private or sensitive information, including:

  • home address, school address, live location, daily schedule, or travel plans;
  • phone number, email, passwords, recovery codes, banking information, payment information, Social Security numbers, ID documents, or account logins;
  • private family information, medical information, therapy information, legal information, or information about another person without permission;
  • photos, videos, or audio that could embarrass, expose, identify, or harm you or another person.

5A. Optional Live Weather location processing

Location notice: The Live Weather theme can request your device's location to retrieve current local weather. Browser/device location requires a separate permission prompt and may provide relatively precise coordinates. You can deny that browser permission and continue using MessageX.

If device location is denied, unavailable, unsupported, or blocked because the page is not running in a secure context, MessageX will use an IP-based approximate-location fallback only when you separately opt in using the optional checkbox below. IP-based location is less accurate and may identify only a city, region, internet-provider area, VPN endpoint, or another nearby area.

When the IP fallback is used, MessageX contacts GeoJS. When MessageX is opened directly as a local file://, it uses GeoJS's documented JSONP endpoint because ordinary cross-origin fetch() requests can be blocked for the local file origin. On a hosted HTTPS build, MessageX may use GeoJS's CORS-enabled JSON endpoint and may contact ipwho.is only if GeoJS fails. Each contacted provider necessarily receives the public IP address used for that request and may return an estimated city, region, country, latitude, longitude, accuracy radius, and timezone. MessageX extracts only the approximate coordinates and a short location label needed to request weather. MessageX does not intentionally save the returned public IP address or the complete provider response.

Weather requests are sent to Open-Meteo using the selected coordinates. MessageX locally caches a limited weather result so the theme can continue during short outages. The cache is sanitized to exclude raw latitude, longitude, and IP-provider response data. The IP fallback consent preference is saved in this browser and can be revoked in Settings. Revoking consent prevents new IP-location requests but cannot undo processing already completed by the third-party provider.

The optional IP fallback is not required to use MessageX. Leaving the optional consent checkbox unchecked means MessageX will not contact the IP-location provider. The Live Weather theme will instead use device location, cached conditions, or a non-location preview.

6. Messages, group chats, and media uploads

You are responsible for the content you send. Do not send or upload anything that is illegal, threatening, harassing, sexually exploitative, hateful, violent, invasive, deceptive, or harmful. Do not pressure anyone to send photos, videos, secrets, passwords, private information, or personal content.

Group chats can increase risk because more people may see, copy, save, or forward messages. Before posting in a group, assume every member could save or share what you send. Do not add people to groups to harass, embarrass, exclude, target, or pressure them.

7. Calls, camera, microphone, and screen sharing

MessageX includes audio calls, video calls, group calls, and screen sharing. These features can reveal more than you expect. Your camera may show your room, face, family members, location clues, school items, documents, or personal belongings. Your microphone may capture background conversations or private sounds. Screen sharing may reveal notifications, messages, apps, photos, tabs, passwords, usernames, files, or private accounts.

  • Only start or accept calls with people you trust.
  • Check your surroundings before turning on camera or microphone.
  • Close private tabs, apps, files, and notifications before screen sharing.
  • Stop screen sharing immediately if private information appears.
  • Do not record, stream, repost, or share someone’s call, voice, screen, or video without permission.

8. Games, polls, reactions, themes, and social features

Games, polls, reactions, status-style features, themes, wallpapers, and profile customization are for fun and communication. Do not use them to bully, target, rank, shame, threaten, exclude, impersonate, trick, or pressure another person. Polls should not be used to humiliate people or collect private information.

9. Respectful use and prohibited behavior

You agree not to use MessageX to:

  • bully, harass, stalk, threaten, blackmail, shame, or intimidate anyone;
  • impersonate another person or pretend to be someone you are not;
  • share private information about someone without permission;
  • send spam, scams, phishing links, malicious files, or deceptive requests;
  • encourage self-harm, violence, illegal activity, substance misuse, exploitation, or dangerous behavior;
  • upload or request sexual, exploitative, abusive, or non-consensual content;
  • try to break, overload, reverse engineer, attack, bypass, scrape, or abuse the app, database, storage, calls, or polling system;
  • use MessageX to coordinate harm, threats, doxxing, swatting, fraud, credential theft, or harassment.

10. Blocking, reporting, moderation, and safety limits

MessageX may include blocking, profiles, presence, and other safety features. These tools can help, but they are not perfect. Blocking may not erase past messages, prevent screenshots, stop someone from using another account, or remove content that other users already saved. The app may not have full-time human moderation or automatic detection for every harmful message.

If someone is threatening you, pressuring you, exposing private information, sending harmful content, or making you feel unsafe, stop engaging, block them if possible, save evidence if needed, and tell a trusted adult, parent/guardian, school staff member, or appropriate authority.

11. Emergency and crisis warning

MessageX is not an emergency service. Do not use MessageX to contact police, fire rescue, emergency medical services, crisis support, or urgent help. If someone may be in immediate danger, use local emergency services or contact a trusted adult right away.

Messages, calls, notifications, polling, internet service, devices, browsers, or the database may fail or be delayed. Do not rely on MessageX for urgent safety needs, medical needs, legal needs, transportation emergencies, or time-sensitive help.

12. Data, storage, deletion, and permanence

Deleting a message, media item, profile detail, call record, game result, or account may not remove every copy. Copies may remain in another user’s device, browser cache, screenshots, downloads, backups, logs, storage buckets, database records, exported data, copied links, forwarded content, admin tools, or older versions of the app. MessageX cannot guarantee that every copy of deleted content disappears everywhere.

The app may store data locally on your device/browser and remotely through its backend. Local storage can include browser localStorage, IndexedDB, caches, media previews, settings, drafts, custom wallpapers, remembered acceptance of these Terms, the optional IP-weather fallback consent preference, or a sanitized recent-weather cache. Remote storage can include database rows, storage bucket files, URLs, metadata, timestamps, sender/recipient information, read status, reactions, call records, and other content needed for the app to function.

Clearing browser data, switching devices, changing browsers, losing internet, database errors, storage errors, policy mistakes, or app bugs may cause data loss, duplicate data, missing messages, old cached data, delayed updates, or accidental exposure. Some data may remain available to other users or administrators even if it no longer appears on your screen.

13. Third-party services and internet risks

MessageX may rely on third-party services such as Supabase, Open-Meteo for weather, GeoJS and—on hosted builds only when GeoJS fails—ipwho.is for the optional IP-based approximate-location fallback, browser APIs, device cameras, microphones, media storage, network providers, fonts, or other web technologies. These services may have their own outages, security rules, privacy practices, limits, or bugs. Your internet connection, browser, device permissions, and operating system settings can affect whether MessageX works correctly.

14. App performance, data usage, and device impact

Calls, video, screen sharing, media uploads, large chat histories, custom wallpapers, games, and frequent polling can use battery, mobile data, storage, memory, and CPU. On slower phones or Android HTML viewers, the app may lag, freeze, crash, reload, or fail to send/receive updates. Use Wi-Fi when possible and avoid uploading large private files.

15. User content and permission to operate the app

You own the content you create, but by sending or uploading content through MessageX, you give the app permission to store, display, transmit, process, resize, cache, and show that content as needed for the app to work. This includes showing messages to recipients, displaying media, showing profile information, processing reactions, enabling games/polls, and supporting calls or screen sharing.

You must have permission to upload or share anything you send. Do not upload content that belongs to someone else unless you are allowed to share it.

16. No guarantee of availability or accuracy

MessageX is provided as-is. Features may break, change, disappear, load slowly, or behave incorrectly. Messages can fail, duplicate, arrive late, show out of order, or be lost. Presence, typing indicators, read receipts, call status, games, polls, and notifications may be inaccurate because they depend on browser/device behavior, database access, and polling timing.

17. Changes to these Terms

The MessageX Terms may be updated when features, risks, safety rules, or technical behavior changes. If the Terms are updated, you may be required to review and accept the updated version before using the app again.

18. If you do not agree

If you do not agree with these Terms, do not use MessageX. Selecting Decline will keep the app locked. You can return later and accept only if you truly agree and meet the age requirement.

This agreement is a safety-focused app template and may need review by a qualified attorney or responsible adult before public release. Laws and platform rules can vary by location, age, audience, and how the app is operated.
Scroll to the bottom of the Terms. The acceptance pop-up will appear when you reach the end.
You must confirm the age/permission statement and agree to the full Terms, including data leak and authenticator/security limits, before entering. Acceptance is saved on this device/browser.

    MessageX Call Labs

    Screen sharing

    During a video call, tap Share and choose a tab, app, or screen. MessageX replaces the outgoing camera track while keeping microphone audio active. When sharing ends, it switches back to the camera automatically.

    Eight-person group calls

    Group calls support eight people total: you plus as many as seven selected participants. The participant counter and picker use the same limit.

    Video quality

    Video targets 1280 × 720 at up to 24 frames per second to balance clarity, bandwidth, battery life, and older-phone performance.

    Microphone reliability

    Calls request echo cancellation, noise suppression, automatic gain control, and mono voice audio when the browser supports those constraints.

    Permissions

    Camera, microphone, and screen sharing require browser permission. If a permission was blocked, re-enable it in the browser site settings and restart the call.

    Mobile guidance

    Use a stable Wi-Fi connection for group video, keep MessageX in the foreground during call startup, and avoid battery-saver modes that suspend browser media.

    Audio playback

    If remote audio is silent, tap Audio in the call controls. Some mobile browsers require a user gesture before they will play sound.

    Leaving a call

    Tap Hang up before closing the tab so the session and participant records are updated immediately.
